default_bullets.text: Meet with Target's officers and management in order to develop an understanding of operations, including its:

children.text: Basis of financial information;
Organization structure;
Historical growth by geography; and
Finance function, financial reporting framework and internal control environment.

default_bullets.text: Read Target's financial statements and discuss Target's accounting policies and practices with management, including:

children.text: Finance function, financial reporting framework, and management reporting relationships (including processes, timing, structure of financial reporting, and interaction with the information/accounting systems);
Significant accounting policies and recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles, procedures, or estimates;
Significant accounting estimates (e.g. bad debt provision, rebates, capitalized R&D, etc.); and
Intercompany accounts and related party transactions, if any.

default_bullets.text: Consider if the following accounting policies have been applied consistently within and across historical periods:

children.text: Cash versus accrual accounting policies;
Capitalization policies;
Depreciation policies; and
Differences between interim and year-end procedures; and
Revenue recognition policies;

default_bullets.text: Identify, and where possible quantify, potential earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization ("EBITDA") normalization items by considering:

children.text: The impact of the normalization adjustments identified by Management;
Large, unusual or non-recurring events, or transactions that may have distorted results;
The impact of any changes to senior management or management team structure;
The impact of provisions (allowance for doubtful accounts, inventory obsolescence, other), management estimates or adjusting entries on the reported results;
The impact of foreign currency transactions;
The impact of related party transactions and shared services provided by other entities, if applicable;
The impact of any changes in accounting policies, procedures and estimates; and
Other potential items identified during the due diligence process.

default_bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of existing and future capital cost requirements including:

children.text: Maintenance versus growth capital expenditures;
Other historical, deferred, and planned capital expenditures.

default_bullets.text: Inquire about significant commitments and contingent liabilities including:

children.text: Pending or threatened litigation or investigations by regulatory or other authorities; and
Contractual obligations, including leases;
Purchase commitments; and
Employee agreements (e.g. transaction/retention bonuses, change-in-control, deferred compensation, or severance agreements, etc.);
Incentive compensation and employee benefit obligations;
Expected or contingent liabilities (e.g. environmental, litigation, regulatory and tax); and
Other off-balance sheet transactions.

default_bullets.text: Summarize and comment on net debt items (on and off-balance sheet) presented by Management and other potential debt-like items;
Consider whether elements of working capital have the nature of and may be reclassified as net debt, and if so, summarize the potential impact of these adjustments on working capital and net debt.
If required, obtain and read the auditor's working papers for the latest fiscal year.
Comment on the nature and volume of audit differences (recorded and unrecorded adjustments), use of accounting estimates, and any changes in accounting policies or methods.
Comment on control issues identified by the external auditors, if applicable.
Comment on any additional red flags or key risks identified in the provided audit materials.
Review audit committee reporting and presentations from the audit firm.

business_overview.text: Gain an understanding of key products and customer segments (deposit, lending, payments, wealth, etc.).
Understand balance sheet composition, funding sources, and interest rate sensitivity drivers.
Understand regulatory environment and reporting requirements applicable to the business.

accounting_overview.text: Understand incremental banking-specific accounting policies and practices, including:

children.text: Fair value measurement policies for financial instruments and related valuation considerations;
Expected credit loss provisioning methodology and key judgments;
Revenue recognition policies for net interest margin (including effective interest treatment);
Deferred placement/origination fees and capitalized commissions (including amortization approach);
Intercompany funding/transfer pricing allocation practices (if applicable); and
Accounting treatment for securitization assets/liabilities and related derecognition considerations (if applicable).

operating_expenses.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's operating expenses and inquire about:

children.text: Employee costs: headcount and staff compensation (salary, payroll tax, bonus, pensions, other benefits, key contractual terms), applicable personnel metrics, and any capitalization of such costs;
Stock options: impact of stock options and other non-cash incentive tools;
Commissions, finders' fees, securitization transaction costs, and related items;
Infrastructure and other technology direct expenses;
Research and development expenses and related capitalization;
Marketing and business development expenses;
Shared services cost allocations and description of services rendered, including allocation of costs within internal line-of-business management reporting, if applicable;
General and administrative costs, including allocation of costs within internal line-of-business management reporting;
Fixed versus variable costs; and
Unusual and extraordinary items.

balance_sheet.text: Obtain and read an analysis of Target's historical balance sheet items and comment on:

children.text: Mortgages, including mortgages under securitization and mortgages held for sale;
Securities;
Restricted assets;
Derivative assets;
Securitization assets and liabilities;
Deposits by category;
Debt and debt-like items, including:
Attribution of debt between funding for operations versus corporate debt;
Off-balance sheet items;
Cash collateralization obligations; and
Repurchase agreement obligations.
Working capital profile including receivables, payables, and accruals; and
Commitments and contingencies.

revenue_analysis.text: Gain an understanding of income streams by type, including:

children.text: Net interest income and margin - trends therein / evolution thereof, including:
Interest rate profile (fixed vs variable; differences by term / duration);
Product features (variable/floating and fixed);
Changes in maturity profile (including extensions of amortization periods and impact on yields); and
Cost of funding.
Non-interest income and non-interest margin - trends therein, key drivers, and one-time / non-cash items.
Other income (including fee income and commissions) - trends therein and noted one-time / non-cash items.

loan_portfolio_and_credit_quality.text: Obtain and analyze loan portfolio composition by product, geography, industry, and risk rating.
Review delinquency, non-performing assets, charge-offs, recoveries, and vintage/loss trends.
Assess concentrations (single-name, sector, geographic) and underwriting/credit policy changes over the Historical Period.

allowance_for_credit_losses.text: Understand allowance methodology, inputs, and model governance (if applicable).
Review qualitative overlays, management adjustments, and sensitivity to macro assumptions.
Reconcile allowance movements over time and assess coverage ratios versus peer/portfolio risk.

regulatory_capital_and_liquidity.text: Understand regulatory capital metrics and drivers (risk-weighted assets, capital buffers) where applicable.
Review liquidity profile and stress testing framework where applicable.
Assess the impact of off-balance sheet exposures and commitments on capital/liquidity.
